content: We can draw an unexpected parallel between human language learner vocabulary reflexion (the core linguistic sense of Reflexion from Topic A) and LLM self-reflection mechanisms: the spaced vocabulary review routines that human learners use to solidify reflected-on new word knowledge can be adapted to improve how large language models consolidate self-reflected error corrections. This framework reframes LLM self-reflection not as a one-off inference step, but as an iterative, human-like learning cycle of encoding, reflecting, and reinforcing insights.
